response:
The best exception rule is **d_metallic_glass**.

The anomaly is that `vitreloy` is not brittle, despite the general rule `r_brittle` predicting that metals are brittle. To explain this without overriding the general rule for all metals (which would affect `steel_a`), we need a more specific rule. `d_metallic_glass` (`amorphous_solid(X) ~> ~brittle(X)`) identifies the specific property of metallic glasses (amorphous solids) that makes them an exception to the general rule for metals.

d_metallic_glass

meta.system:
Pick the single best exception rule. Candidates:
d_metallic_glass: amorphous_solid(X) ~> ~brittle(X)
d_mg_broad: metal(X
meta.user:
Facts: material(steel_a), material(vitreloy), metal(steel_a), metal(vitreloy).
General rules (=> means "normally implies"): r_brittle: metal(X) => brittle(X); r_conduct: metal(X) => conducts_electrici
